paper.title: Multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1) and type 4 (MEN4)

entities.name: SUV39H1
synonyms: suppressor of variegation 3–9 homolog family protein

entities.name: Multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1) and type 4 (MEN4)

evidence_entities.text: Multiple endocrine neoplasia (MEN) is characterized by the occurrence of tumors involving two or more endocrine glands within a single patient
MEN1 is characterized by the occurrence of parathyroid, pancreatic islet and anterior pituitary tumors
MEN2 is characterized by the occurrence of medullary thyroid carcinoma (MTC) in association with phaeochromocytoma and parathyroid tumors

MEN3 is characterized by the occurrence of MTC and phaeochromocytoma in association with a marfanoid habitus, mucosal neuromas, medullated corneal fibers and intestinal autonomic ganglion dysfunction, leading to megacolon
MEN4 is characterized by the occurrence of parathyroid and anterior pituitary tumors in possible association with tumors of the adrenals, kidneys, and reproductive organs
The gene causing MEN1 is located on chromosome 11q13, and encodes a 610 amino-acid protein, menin
menin has functions in cell division, genome stability, and transcription regulation
MEN4 is caused by heterozygous mutations of CDNK1B which encodes the 196 amino-acid CDK1 p27Kip1
